title: Understanding the Forces
text: To find the force exerted by the motorcycle, consider both the force needed to overcome resistance and the force needed to accelerate the motorcycle.

title: Calculate the Net Force
text: Use Newton's second law to calculate the net force required to accelerate the motorcycle. The formula is $$F=ma$$, where F is the force, $$m$$ is the mass, and a is the acceleration.

title: Calculate the Force for Acceleration
text: Calculate the force needed to accelerate the motorcycle using the formula $$F=ma$$. Given: $$m=245$$ kg and $$a=3.50$$ $$\frac{m}{s^2}$$.

title: Total Force Exerted by the Motorcycle
text: The total force exerted by the motorcycle is the sum of the force needed to overcome resistance and the force needed for acceleration.

title: Calculate the Total Force
text: Add the force needed to overcome resistance (400 N) to the force needed for acceleration $$(857.5$$ N).
